formality.description: Russian has a clear T-V distinction: вы (vy, formal/plural) vs ты (ty, informal/singular). Professional software uses Вы-form (often capitalized for single addressee in writing). The formal register uses longer sentence constructions and avoids colloquialisms.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Russian has grammatical gender on nouns, adjectives, and past-tense verbs. There is no widely adopted gender-neutral writing convention. When gender is unknown, masculine is the conventional default. Where possible, restructure to avoid gendered forms.

formal-vy.label: Вы (Formal)
formal-vy.description: Standard for professional software, enterprise, and institutional contexts.
formal-vy.prompt: Formal Russian. Use Вы-form (capitalized for single addressee). Professional register appropriate for software interfaces, enterprise, and business communication. Avoid colloquialisms and informal expressions.

informal-ty.label: Ты (Informal)
informal-ty.description: For consumer apps, social media, gaming, and casual brand voices.
informal-ty.prompt: Informal Russian. Use ты-form. Conversational, friendly tone for consumer products, social media, and gaming. Colloquial expressions acceptable when natural.
